entry.title: Assembly data cant be found in Work center POD.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to highlight an issue, which Im facing on Assembly data in POD. As seen in Image01, I cannot see any data inputs even after I followed the steps as shown in this  Blog. 

I created first Data fields >> Data types (Category: Assembly/Consumption) >> Manage material (Data to Collect at Assembly/Consumption).

Data typeManage MaterialsAssembly Data in Work POD

Could anyone help me out, how to solve this thing.

Thanks and Regards,
Sayuj.

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content: Then why Sy-subrc = 0 always.?.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad "Cannot load tile"
entry.content: This worked for me! Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Scripting Tracker - SAP Window not found
entry.content:
Hi @stefan_schnell,

Before I submitted my question to the community, I verified that all GUI settings are setup for scripting to function in that specific SAP system.  I actually did a side-by-side comparison with another system that the tracker works in and all settings are identical.  Thanks for the verification check.

entry.title: Re: Scripting Tracker - SAP Window not found
entry.content: Hi Thomas. I've got the SAP system open that I'm trying to use the scripting tracker in. I don't have a script running and I'm not actually doing anything in the window actively. I'm just trying to use the scripting tracker as it works in many other SAP systems I work in. I've just never seen the scripting tracker tool not "see" an SAP system before. That's what I'm lost with. The tracker tool is great and will help me with my scripting efforts, but the tool is currently not working with a specific SAP system (I have multiple that I use and others work just fine). Thanks for your input.

entry.title: Re: Problem with ALV column displaying as exponent instea...
entry.content:
Solution: I created a DDIC structure that matched the projection list of the CDS View and selected into an internal table of that type. This resolved the output of the value on the ALV grid. I was selecting into an internal table using dynamic typing.

Select * from abc into table @DATA(gt_output)

Was the problem, the solution

data: gt_output type standard table of <my_ddic_struc> .

Select * from abc into corresponding-fields of table @GT_output

entry.title: Re: How to pass escape key in SAP iRPA
entry.content: Hello Chaytanya, I am currently trying to pass the CTRL + SHIFT + 6 using the keystroke element activity from SAP Build Process Automation, but it's not working as expected. Do you have any advice on how to effectively use this keystroke within this context? Any insights or tips you could provide would be very helpful as I am looking to enhance my automation processes. Thank you for your help. Best regards,

entry.title: How do stop the proration of WCB prorated assesable earnings when there is a change in company code
entry.content: Is there a way to suppress proration when there is a change in company codes?

entry.title: Using Ragas with AI core + other metrics to evaluate LLMs
entry.content:
Challenges: LLMs must be evaluated for accuracy before they are deployed. It is difficult to qualify the accuracy simply by observation, especially for complicated tasks involving Retrieval Augmented Generation or translation/summarization. These metrics discussed in this blog post provide quantitative scores for various types of tasks involving large language models.

In this post, we will take a look at 

  • Ragas
  • Bleu & Rouge
  • Deepeval benchmarks.

Ragas

Ragas is a tool which is used for evaluating accuracy of LLMs in RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) applications.

With RAG applications, testing must be fully done before deployment is a possibility. RAGas simplifies that testing process by providing overall scores for various metrics which measure's your LLMs ability to perform retrieval tasks, get the right context, and provide the right answers.

Ragas requires you to create your own dataset with your own questions and answers. This requires that you must know or be able to figure out the correct answer to these questions in order for Ragas to make the comparison. The dataset must have the following 4 columns:

question | answer | contexts | ground_truth

where answer is the response from the LLM for a given question, contexts is the context/source in which the answer was found in (usually given by a similarity search), and ground_truth is the actual/expected answer.

Ragas uses several metrics to evaluate the accuracy and reliability of LLM responses. 

faithfulness - evaluated by determining if the answer provided by the LLM can be inferred from the given context.

answer relevance - determines if the original question can be reconstructed from the given answer.

context precision - For each chunk in context, checks if it's relevant to arrive to the ground truth. Checks if chunks which are most relevant to the ground_truth appear at the top (as they should be).

context recall - checks each sentence in the ground truth and determines if it is attributable to the context.

There are also 5 more additional metrics that can be used, which are documented here 

Integrate and run ragas test with SAP AI Core.

In this example app, we have prepared 15 questions, ran each one on Gemini, Claude, and GPT LLMs, and will run ragas tests on all 3 to compare the results.

The data has columns question, contexts, ground_truth, and an answer column for each llm.

Here is an outline of how the data should look like:

*note* Normally you would have only one "answer" column, but in this particular case we are wanting to compare the performance of 3 different large language models.

Ragas will need to use an LLM in order to perform the evaluation test itself, you can use any LLM of your choice. In this example we are using a GPT4 llm which is deployed in AI core. 

An text embedding model will also be needed to perform the test, we will use an AI core deployment of text embedding ada 002.

Required Libraries:

  • SAP generative AI hub SDK (pip install generative-ai-hub-sdk)
  • SAP AI core SDK (pip install ai-core-sdk)
  • RAGas (pip install ragas)

Env file

a .env file must be made in the same directory as the app file with the following values

 

 

AICORE_CLIENT_ID=
AICORE_AUTH_URL=
AICORE_CLIENT_ID=
AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET=
AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP=
AICORE_BASE_URL=
OPENAI_API_KEY=

 

Imports

*Note*: You can list any metrics you want to use in the ragas test under the import block as displayed in lines 8-14. The ones currently listed are the default metrics, if you choose to omit this import and not provide any metrics list, these are the ones that will be used regardless.

 

 

from ai_core_sdk.ai_core_v2_client import AICoreV2Client
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_llm
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_embedding_model

from datasets import Dataset
import pandas as pd
from ragas import evaluate
from ragas.metrics import (
    faithfulness,
    answer_relevancy,
    context_relevancy,
    context_recall,
    context_precision
)
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

 

Loading AI Core credentials and initializing chat completion and text embedding LLMs.

These credentials come from the service key of your AI core instance in BTP

 

 

client = AICoreV2Client(base_url="BASE_URL",
                        auth_url="AUTH_URL",
                        client_id="CLIENT_ID",
                        client_secret="CLIENT_SECRET",
                        resource_group="default")

langchain_llm = init_llm('gpt-4', max_tokens=100)
embeddings = init_embedding_model('text-embedding-ada-002')

 

Creating datasets for Ragas

Our data is currently in an excel csv file, however we must format this data into a dataset before we run the ragas test.

The 1st step is to create a dict for each of our 3 LLMs.

We will then use the pandas library to read each column in the excel file and use them to populate our dictionaries.

Finally, we create a Dataset from those dicts.

 

df = pd.read_csv('Q&A_DATA.csv')
df['contexts'] = df['contexts'].apply(lambda x: [x])

gemini_data["question"] = df['question'].tolist()
gemini_data["answer"] =        df['gemini_answer'].tolist()
gemini_data["contexts"] =      df['contexts'].tolist()
gemini_data["ground_truth"] =  df['ground_truth'].tolist()

claude_data["question"] = df['question'].tolist()
claude_data["answer"] =        df['claude_answer'].tolist()
claude_data["contexts"] =      df['contexts'].tolist()
claude_data["ground_truth"] =  df['ground_truth'].tolist()

gpt_data["question"] = df['question'].tolist()
gpt_data["answer"] =        df['gpt_answer'].tolist()
gpt_data["contexts"] =      df['contexts'].tolist()
gpt_data["ground_truth"] =  df['ground_truth'].tolist()

gemini_dataset = Dataset.from_dict(gemini_data)
claude_dataset = Dataset.from_dict(claude_data)
gpt_dataset = Dataset.from_dict(gpt_data)

 

 *Note* line 2 is mandatory, as it ensures the contexts property in the dict will be an array.

Finally, we run the tests on ragas using the imported "evaluate" function:

 

 

gemini_result = evaluate(
    dataset=gemini_dataset,
    llm=langchain_llm,
    embeddings=embeddings
)

claude_result = evaluate(
    dataset=claude_dataset,
    llm=langchain_llm,
    embeddings=embeddings
)

gpt_result = evaluate(
    dataset=gpt_dataset,
    llm=langchain_llm,
    embeddings=embeddings
)

print("Gemini result " + str(gemini_result) + '\n\n')
print("Claude result " + str(claude_result) + '\n\n')
print("Gpt result" + str(gpt_result) + '\n\n')

 

And print our results

 

Gemini result {'answer_relevancy': 0.9378, 'context_precision': 1.0000, 'faithfulness': 1.0000, 'context_recall': 1.0000}

Claude result {'answer_relevancy': 0.9462, 'context_precision': 1.0000, 'faithfulness': 0.5833, 'context_recall': 1.0000}

Gpt result{''answer_relevancy': 0.8625, 'context_precision': 1.0000, 'faithfulness': 1.0000, 'context_recall': 1.0000}

 

Bleu & Rouge (Non-rag metrics)

Bleu, Rouge

Bleu is a metric which is normally used to evaluate a model's ability to translate text, while Rouge is used to evaluate its ability to summarize or capture the meaning of text.

Rouge

When using the Rouge metric to measure the quality of a LLM generated summary, you are to provide a reference summary (created by you or another human) showing what would be an example of a good summary. The Rouge metric will be calculated by comparing the llm generated summary with the human-provided one.

Different methods of Rouge:

  • Rouge-1. This counts how many matching words there are between the human and machine generated summary
  • Rouge-2 This counts how many matching word pairs there are between the human and machine generated summary
  • Rouge-L This finds the longest common subsequence between the human and machine generated summary. The sequence does not have to be contiguous, just in the same order.

Code example:

 

from datasets import load_metric 
rouge = load_metric("rouge") 
llmSummaries = ["I was in walmart yesterday"] 
humanSummary = ["I went to walmart yesterday"]
print(rouge.compute(predictions=llmSummaries, references=humanSummary))

 

Console output:

 

{'rouge1': AggregateScore(low=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), mid=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), high=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6)), 

'rouge2': AggregateScore(low=Score(precision=0.25, recall=0.25, fmeasure=0.25), mid=Score(precision=0.25, recall=0.25, fmeasure=0.25), high=Score(precision=0.25, recall=0.25, fmeasure=0.25)), 

'rougeL': AggregateScore(low=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), mid=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), high=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6)), 

'rougeLsum': AggregateScore(low=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), mid=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6), high=Score(precision=0.6, recall=0.6, fmeasure=0.6))}

 

We are provided with rouge1, rouge2, and rougeL scores

Bleu

Similiar to Rouge, you must provide a human reference translation as well as the machine generated one you wish to get a Bleu score for. 

Bleu works by getting the unigram precision. Which it obtains by comparing the machine and human generated translations, counting the number of word matches, and dividing it by the number of words in the generation. It does not count consecutively repeated words as to avoid giving sentences with only the same word repeated a perfect score. It also has the option of comparing matching n-pair words instead of just one. During the calculation of the score, it will compare with both single pair and n-pair words to account for sentence order (as this can be different between languages).

Example:

 

from datasets import load_metric

bleu = load_metric("bleu")
llmTranslation = [["I", "am", "of", "Spain"]]
humanTranslations = [
    [["I", "am", "from", "Spain"], ["I'm", "from", "Spain"]]
]
print(bleu.compute(predictions=llmTranslation, references=humanTranslations))

 

output:

 

bleu = load_metric("bleu")
{'bleu': 0.0, 'precisions': [0.75, 0.3333333333333333, 0.0, 0.0], 'brevity_penalty': 1.0, 'length_ratio': 1.3333333333333333, 'translation_length': 4, 'reference_length': 3}

 

 

Deepeval Benchmarks

Deepeval is an LLM evluation framework developed by confident-ai which provides access to various benchmark tests that can be used to evaluate a model’s overall performance. 

Unlike the above metrics, these benchmarks evaluate the performance of a model by using entire datasets.  Each benchmark represents a different dataset containing a list of relevant questions and expected answers (ground truths) for the task.

  • HellaSwag
  • TruthfulQ&A
  • MMLU
  • DROP

HellaSwag - Provides 10,000 challenges revolving around sentence completion.

DROP - 9,500 challenges which measure a model's reasoning abilities. 

TruthfulQ&A - 817 challenges (questions) which determine if a model is able to answer questions truthfully. Common misconceptions (things believed by many people which are not actually true) are a key part of these challenges.

MMLU - 15,000 multiple choice challenges ranging from various topics (57 different subjects). I.e. Math, history, law, and ethics

All these benchmarks are available in the deepeval library (pip install deepeval). Each benchmark has a set of tasks associate with it, and you can choose which tasks you want to include.

An example with MMLUTask

 

 

import torch
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForCausalLM
from deepeval.benchmarks import MMLU
from deepeval.benchmarks.tasks import MMLUTask

benchmark = MMLU(
    tasks=[MMLUTask.FORMAL_LOGIC, MMLUTask.GLOBAL_FACTS],
    n_shots=3
)

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained("Jimmyhd/testRepo3",
                                             device_map='auto',
                                             torch_dtype=torch.float16,
                                             load_in_4bit=True,
                                             bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
                                           bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,
                                             trust_remote_code=True)
benchmark.evaluate(model=model)
print(benchmark.overall_score)

 

**Note** the "trust_remote_code=True" parameter in the AutmoModelForCasualLlm call is mandatory in order to use any of the benchmarks.

For more detail about how the benchmarks are evaluated and different examples using them, see the documentation here: 

Conclusion

Whether you want to evaluate a model’s ability to perform rag functions, translate, summarize, reason, or just answer questions based on common mathematical and scientific knowledge, there are many different metric and benchmark tools which will provide you a quantitative score that will allow you to see just how well the model is performing before your application deployed, giving you full assurance. 

This will conclude our look at these 3 types of evaluations!

entry.title: TVRF and PPF process for deal approval
entry.content:
Hello Team

 

  1. I am registering a PCA TVRF for a customer following the process from the below.

 

 

  1. However, its takes me to the below page which when furnished triggers this email to TDD asking me to take up the test, when I already have access to workzone.

 

  1. Kindly advise on the next steps I have already created the folders ad below on the workzone. What is the next step to submit once the Technical viability review form is filled by the PCA(me) and to proceed for deal approval, they need to submit the form to trigger notification to the TDD/IAE/PBM/PCA. Please advise.

Thanks,

 

PRADEEP DEVADAS

Principal Consultant – PCA

entry.title: Account assignment object doesn't display in warehouse tasks SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi, experts.

I try to config cost center goods issue process in SAP EWM.

1.MB21 : Create reservation(movement type 201)
2.MIGO : Post goods issue refer to the reservation number.
         →outbound delivery is created.
3./SCWM/PRDO : Create warehouse order & warehouse task refer to the outbound delivery number.
4./SCWM/MON : Check the outbound delivery item & warehouse task.

I would like to check cost center code in those documents, but, I can check it in the outbound delivery only.

In the outbound delivery, I can check it in the "Account assignment object".

That item also exists in the warehouse task, but the item is blank space.

■outbound delivery

■warehouse task

Is it a system failure? Or is it a lack of some settings?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards.

So Adachi.

entry.title: Re: First SAP Tech Night Melbourne on 13th June 2024
entry.content: Looking forward to it!! @Saikrishna_Sriram

entry.title: Interested in Digital Skills Initiative Opportunity For SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Anna T. I've just joined the SAP group. I'm very excited to get started on my learning initiative journey and to have the opportunity to participate in the digital skills initiative.

As a woman in the tech industry with experience in business processes, agile methodology, quality assurance, data insights and business analysis in cloud software implementations, particularly in the insurance software industry, I think the digital skills initiative would be a wonderful opportunity to broaden my industry knowledge, join the world of SAP and learn more about the products SAP offers. SAP is a whole new world with lots of opportunities and I want to uplift and support others, especially those who are underrepresented in the industry. I think that is critical to make the tech industry more accessible for everyone.

I would love to have the opportunity to join the Digital Skills Initiative. It would give me a great opportunity to broaden my horizons with SAP and apply myself into the SAP ecosystem. 

I'm particularly interested in getting started with this learning journey below:
Explore Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA

I have my private messenger enabled on my settings in my account.

Thank you so much for this opportunity and for your help with my private message settings on my account!

Thank you,

Anna T

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ - Post Actions not executed
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't think it is as designed that post action will be not executed if the user re-triggers the action after it fails for the first time. If you could reproduce the issue stably, I would recommend you raise an SAP ticket to SAP CPQ component with detailed reproducing steps. The dev team will help you with the root cause if it is identified as a bug.

Best regards
Fancy

entry.title: History of User's log on information
entry.content:
Hi,

We've received inquiries concerning the user's log-on history. I understand that SAP BTP records the user's most recent login date and time. But, I'm uncertain whether SAP BTP retains this historical information. For instance, we're interested in finding out if SAP BTP keeps track of this data. For instance, we're looking to answer the following questions:

  1. How many times USER A has logged into the system last week.
  2. How many times USER B has logged into the system today.
  3. How many Users logged into the system for a given period (e.g. say Yesterday 10:00 am to 14:00pm )

I searched for this data in both IAS where we have configured the Azure AD( SSO ) and ABAP BTP. In each location, I could only find the date and time of the last login, but no past records. 

Can you confirm if this information is available and if so, how to access these data?

Thanks

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: History of User's log on information
entry.content:
You may find usage statistics for IAS using Reporting as explained in the following link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/view-usage-statistics

If you need to check logon history for users, you may use audit logs. Please check the following link for more information:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/audit-logs

entry.title: Use Persona Flavor for SQ01 for Fiori
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a Ztcode with SQ01 report behind it.

Then I created Persona flavor for this Ztcode, and the intent is to have users to access it using the Fiori launchpad (Slipstream). I can see the tile in the Fiori launchpad, but somehow it is displaying the original instead of the flavor version, although it seems to try using the flavor such as:

https://xxxxxxx.com/sap/bc/ui2/flp#Slipstream-launch?transaction=ZLIST&flavor=005056B19D401EDF89EDCFCB55145AAE

Any suggestions on what I need to change or try doing next?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Slow in searching service call due to huge records
entry.content: SAP B1 V10 FP2011 HANA

entry.title: Re: Extending SAP Fiori elements the right way using standard processes
entry.content:
Very good and it is helpful for every developer.  Thanks for sharing.
Could you share one example with expression binding to hide Fiori Elements Table columns dynamically.
I have posted the same question here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-hide-table-columns-dynamically-in-fiori-elements-analytical-list/qaq-p/13728111 
Thank you.

entry.title: BRFPLus in Development Client, but would need to test in 300
entry.content:
Hi Community

I not able to find my personal notes how i have resolved this in the past

I have created a BRFPlus Customizing table/Application in 110 client.
The testing is done in 300, 
The transport is a workbench. While testing in 300, i get an exception :


The BRFplus application is not available in 300 too. 
Can you please help with this query
I have used FDT Helpers in the past to package the BRFPlus but to transport to QA later to Production, this time i am lost
SCC1 does not work, since this is a workbench request

entry.title: Re: Deadlock in SAP B1 Database...On ONNM and NNM1
entry.content: I am experiencing same issue can any one recommend a solution please

entry.title: Re: Can we set CFL fields to display only in standard fio...
entry.content:
Hi Unni, 

In the latest releases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud there is now an option in F1481 Custom Fields app called Visibility Control and Behavior where this is possible now for some apps. You will see extra options in the Enable Usage tab if this is provided for the app.

However given that Manage Billing Documents is one of the older apps, it's likely that it does not support this. In which case you would need a developer to create an adaptation project to add that logic

Best

Jocelyn

entry.title: Re: Sub-contracting MRP area not showing in MD04
entry.content:
1. Pls check if the Deletion indicator checked.

 

2. Pls check the material status both in MRP1 view and Basic data view, and compare the two materials you mentioned.

For the material status, it can block the MRP.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Find HXM workforce Planning Package in SAC
entry.content:
Hi @zebashah - the package you are asking about has been updated with the Q2 release of SAC. Unfortunately, we needed to delist it temporarily due to some technical issues that arose. We hope to have this resolved and have the package back online soon!

Please note, as part of this update, the package is also being renamed to "Operational Workforce Planning". Once it is back online in the Content Network, you will find it by this new name.

Apologies for the inconvenience.

Mike

entry.title: Re: Custom field from timesheet to pricing for customer p...
entry.content:
Hello Girar,

If the custom fields could be extend in the app Manage sales rate and also could be enabled for Condition Record for Pricing in Sales.

I think it will be possible to work. You could do this according to the blog and publish the custom filed as checkbox instand of CodeList.

entry.title: Re: How to transfer data from HANADB to snowflake using D...
entry.content:
Use the "Read HANA Table" operator or any other method you prefer to extract data from the ACDOCA table in SAP HANA DB.

If you need to transform the data before loading it into Snowflake (e.g., data cleansing, formatting changes), you can do so using Data Intelligence's transformation capabilities.

Then you can load the data to Snowflake and ensure the connection is established between data intelligence and Snowflake.

If you're still encountering issues with the Cloud Table Producer operator, you can consider alternative methods for loading data into Snowflake using Snowflake bulk loader and snow pipe which can load continuously the file data stored on cloud location.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Good day, @David_Chaviano

I'm a Vietnamese developer for SAP ABAP that was born and raised in a mountainous region. I am extremely excited about technology and its ability to close the opportunity gap for people living in underserved areas like mine, as I am a first-generation college graduate in my family. My ambition to further refine my abilities and knowledge within the SAP ecosystem has been fueled by my experience with ABAP development.

Growing up in a rural place has come with its own special set of difficulties, such as having less access to job and educational options. Notwithstanding these challenges, I'm dedicated to lifelong learning and development. I really feel that earning a certification will enable me to have a significant impact on my community and legitimize my skills.

I'm excited to continue learning and growing within the SAP community. I'm grateful for this initiative and the chance to prove that talent knows no boundaries. Thank you for considering my application for a free certification attempt.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Text Determination in Sales Area of S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: @wagener-mark This feature should be available in the 2023 version

entry.title: no authorization posting Functional area YB50
entry.content:
Hi ,

I want to know why finance user has no authorization to posting a functional area? Need I add a role for him ? Or some other settings?

entry.title: What is the reason why the system state I0270 WCRE is triggered?
entry.content: 在sap下达生产订单时，由于第一道工序里面有I0270 WCRE的系统状态导致订单无法下达。但是不清楚是什么原因导致这个状态被触发的，请问有人知道嘛？ SAP下达生产订单时，由于第一次操作中I0270 WCRE的系统状态，无法下达订单。但我不知道是什么导致了这种状态被触发，有人知道吗？

entry.title: iOS Builds Fail to Export
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I recently migrated my project from AppGyver to SAP Build App and encountered an issue when trying to export the iOS version of my app. Despite my efforts to resolve it, the same error keeps occurring. I've attached a screenshot of the error message for reference.

Steps I've Taken So Far:

  1. Migrated the project from AppGyver to SAP Build App.
  2. Attempted to export the iOS app.
  3. Encountered the error (see attached).
  4. Created a new certificate and mobile provision.
  5. Tried exporting again but faced the same error.

Has anyone faced a similar issue or could provide guidance on how to resolve this? Any help would be greatly appreciated as this is an urgent matter.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: I have a question sap ecc related can anyone give me answer.question SD related
Re: Introduction of SAC Multi-actions Public API
entry.content:
Hi, @hannes2k , @BI2001 ,

Sorry for late response. I also noticed "SystemUser" will be deprecated in BTP destination. Meanwhile, I think BTP designation supports user propagation via JWT token which was illustrated  User Propagation via SAML 2.0 Bearer Assertion Flow | SAP Help Portal

I'm trying out the options mentioned in above link and can share after figuring out some working samples. Meanwhile you may also take that link as a reference.

Thanks,

Manu

entry.title: Withholding Tax in Manage Supplier Line Items
entry.content:
Dear all

 

When I have inputed W/Tax Base and WTax Amt in "Create Incoming Invoice" , I inquire about this item for which from Menu > Document > Display , Withholding Tax is displayed on Display Document: Line Item screen.

However, when I inquire about same Item in "Manage Supplier Line Items", Withholding Tax is displayed as 0.

Please tell me how to display Withholding Tax in "Manage Supplier Line Items".

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Cash based Ledger
entry.content:
thank you for sharing very helpful.  We have customer is asking "How would the system pick up fixed asset related transactions for recording in the cash ledger / cash book under cash basis of accounting, given that the account debited is different for accrual and cash basis?

      • In accrual accounting, the entries are Dr Fixed Asset Cr Cash, while in cash accounting, entries are Dr Expense Cr Cash.  We would like to highlight that for the accrual accounting entries, there will be 2 steps in the process, i.e. Dr Fixed Asset Cr Payable (when PV is raised), and Dr Payable Cr Cash (when PV is paid).  For cash accounting, the same question as Q2b needs to be addressed on posting a single entry, i.e. Dr Expense Cr Cash. 

thanks

entry.title: Re: Replacement Process in Advanced Returns Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Akshay,

Thanks for your sharing, it is very helpful. Is it possible to collaborate replacement PO with Ariba SCC? interact between S/4 HANA and Ariba, supplier can receive replacement in Ariba, and confirm it and create ASN in Ariba, after that information transferred back to S4 system and update the PO confirmation tab and create Inbound delivery automatically. 

As you mentioned above, System automatically creates Inbound Delivery against Replacement PO, can we change this configuration (trigger point) about the automatically inbound creation, let it trigger by ASN from Ariba?  is it possible, if yes, how to do?  

looking forward to your feedback, many thanks in advance!

Hi @Eldhos 

Data Store is not supported for global sites at this moment, please see the Help.

entry.title: Re: Read file path from source SFTP : SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Sri

I've a requirement to read incoming as well as outgoing filename. Each transfer occurs over SFTP.

I've used ${file:name} to capture Incoming filename, and ${header.CamelFileName} to capture Outgoing filename. But both captured the input filename only.

Is there any other property which can be used to capture Outgoing filename.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Scripting Tracker - SAP Window not found
entry.content:
Hello @BPatterson,

thanks for your reply. All seems to be well, without Scripting Tracker is finding one SAP GUI session.

Please open in the Tracker the menu item Tools > Running Object Table (ROT)...

A dialog should open showing two entries for each open session. One SAPFRONT and one SAPFRONT-... SAPGUI must also be visible.

Here an example with four open sessions in two connections.

Are the two entries (SAPFRONT and SAPFRONT-...) visible for all open sessions in your case?

Thanks and best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Convert Json format date to SAP readable format
entry.content:
Hi,
I am executing methods/URI's in Odata(SEGW) and I am getting dates in Json format Unix/Epoch Format : “\/Date(1567981296000)\/”.
How do i convert this to an SAP readable format like MM/DD/YYYY?
I googled this but could not find a proper straight fwd answer. I am getting '0 'as the answers for some of the googled ABAP codes.

Pls suggest.

entry.title: Re: Convert JSON Date from external OData to YYYYMMDD in ABAP Program

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Sample Content for LoB Finance
entry.content:
Thank you for your valuable posting.

1) Is it possible to download other data artifacts, such as views and data flows, within the Finance Sample Data for SAP Data Warehouse Cloud? The Finance Sample Data for SAP Data Warehouse Cloud only contains 18 tables. It would be very helpful if I could access SQL views, text views, time dimensions, and other elements mentioned in your blog.

 

2)  I am encountering an issue where the pandas dt.year function is not working correctly within the SAP Datasphere data flow. Could you advise me on how to resolve this?

Thank you for your assistance.

--

### convert the Date to dateti
data['DATE'] = pd.to_datetime(data['DATE'])
# add a column for Year
data['YEAR'] = data['DATE'].dt.year

return data

--

Best regards,

Hongguen

entry.title: Re: Merge DPRs into one invoice
entry.content:
Hi Horatiu,

Thats a Great question You are right, down payment requests seem like a different story than regular invoices. There might be accounting reasons for keeping them separate, like tracking project funds. Plus, separate invoices offer a clearer record for everyone involved. The SAP Community has some resources that might be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Change of email address or phoneNumber is triggering...

entry.title: Re: How to hide table columns dynamically in Fiori Elemen...
entry.content:
Hi Venkatesh,

The behavior you see is by design. It is not possible to influence this. This is also documented: see for example the "Note" under "Hiding Table Columns Using the UI.Hidden Annotation" in https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/c0f6592a592e47f9bb6d09900de47412.

Best Regards,

     Sandeep

entry.title: Re: Request for digital skills initiative by SAP Learning
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano Hello there, appreciate your efforts. I have turned on my private chats. SO please share the invite.
Thank You

entry.title: Re: Assign multiple Sales Employee to a Business Partner,...
entry.content:
Hello!

Out-of-the-box your options are pretty much to do a UDF (user-defined field) and then link it to the system Sales Employee object like this:

You could add as many as you wanted to any of the system objects, BP, SO, etc.

Then you would build queries to support the multiple commissions.  There isn't much in the way of commission tracking OOB.  It's up to you to track the items, programs, calculations and payments.  I end up doing this for every implementation.

But every business handles commission in a slightly different way so it usually works out OK.

You could use B1UP to make things look nicer (place the fields, automate some of the tracking and data entry) but as long as you make the right fields and tag them properly you should be able to do what you need.

Good luck!

M

entry.title: Re: Decimal separator as comma(,) for numbers in WebI4.3...
entry.content: Hi Amit, the datatype is integer not a string and hence is throwing error when applying replace() function.

entry.title: Re: Can we automate SAP GUI MRS Planning board Gantt Chat using SAP GUI Scripting API's ?
entry.content:
Hello @sapusertest1,

here an excerpt from the SAP GUI Scripting help in the GuiGraphAdaptObject:

"In addition to the new, activeX based controls SAP GUI also comes with a set of external graphics executables, for example to display a GANTT chart. These executables are not supported within the API. If during the execution of a script one of these executables is launched, then the script will be blocked."

As far as I understand it correctly, it is not possible to automate a GANTT chart with SAP GUI Scripting.

The SAP GUI Scripting help continues to say:

"If you need to automate a process during which a graphics executable is displayed, then you need an automation tool then allows you to both manipulate SAP GUI using the Scripting API, and other Windows applications using native methods."

Perhaps a Robotics Process Automation (RPA) approach can help you here.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Streamlining data entry: Exploring the new 'Paste' feature in Fiori Elements V4 tables
entry.content: Thank you, Fabio, we could just benefit from it.

entry.title: Special stock V 100125462 at customer 1000005577 does not exist
entry.content:
Special stock V 100125462 at customer 1000005577 does not exist

Message no. M7185

Diagnosis

No special stock V 100125462 exists at customer 1000005577 (batch 0001657040)

Procedure

Make sure your entries are correct.

Do you want to display the stock overview?

entry.title: Down payment with Resource related billing not considering in DP91
entry.content:
Hi,
 
We are in process of establishing process Downpayment with Resource related billing.
Requirement is Initially against project customer will provide the downpayment and when doing the billing with reference to resource consumption downpayment should get deducted and either debit or credit memo get issued.
I.e
VF01 Downpayment received 100,000 USD
1st month Actual cost consumption on project 50,000 USD
DP91 RRB execution – Cr Memo 50,000 USD (100,000-50,000)
2nd Month Actual cost consumption on project 90,000 USD
DP91 RRB Execution – Dr Memo 40,000 USD
 
We have gone through following notes for completing required configuration for given scenario.
858702 - Consulting note for resource-related down payments
858703 - Customizing for resource-related down payment
983961 - Customizing for resource-related down payment (2)
 
But we are not getting expected result when we are executing DP91. Only actual cost is coming as output and downpayment is not getting considered.
 
Is anyone gone through this scenario and getting any result ? Please provide if you any solution to achieve this process.

entry.title: Re: Use Persona Flavor for SQ01 for Fiori
entry.content: The first step should be checking if everything to the smallest detail in your FLP is configured exactly as described in the SAP Fiori Launchpad Guide for Slipstream Engine.

entry.title: Run Turi Create (or any x86_64 lib) on Apple Silicon architecture
entry.content:
Please note, below outlined steps would apply for any x86_64 based python library to be installed on Apple silicon systems (M1, M2, M3).


If you’ve attempted to install Turi Create using pip install -U turicreate on an Apple Silicon-based system, you may have encountered the following error:

 

==================================================================================
    TURICREATE ERROR

    If you see this message, pip install did not find an available binary package
    for your system.

    Supported Platforms:
        * macOS 10.12+ x86_64.
        * Linux x86_64 (including WSL on Windows 10).

    Support Python Versions:
        * 2.7
        * 3.5
        * 3.6
        * 3.7
        * 3.8

    Another possible cause of this error is an outdated pip version. Try:
        `pip install -U pip`

==================================================================================

 

You may be encountering this issue due to two reasons:

  1. You’re using a Python version not supported by Turi Create, i.e., greater than 3.8 or less than 2.7.
  2. Apple Silicon-based systems use the arm64 architecture type, which is not supported by Turi Create.

To address this, you can create a conda environment that can run the supported architecture type (x86_64) with a supported Python version. Here’s how to do it:

Setting Architecture Type to x86_64

Open a new terminal and run the following command:

env /usr/bin/arch -x86_64 /bin/zsh

This ensures that all subsequent commands in the session will run as the required architecture type. You can also create an alias for this command for future use.

Install Miniconda

Install miniconda using homebrew:

brew install --cask miniconda

If you need to install homebrew, refer the steps at https://brew.sh/

Ensure that Miniconda is installed only after setting the architecture type to x86_64 on your terminal.

Setup Conda environment

Run following commands in the same terminal session.

conda create -n <env_name> -y
conda activate <env_name>
conda config --env --set subdir osx-64

Replace <env_name> with your desired environment name.

Install Supported Python Version

Install a supported Python version for the conda environment:

conda install python=3.10

This will install an x86_64 based python.

Install Turi Create

Finally, install Turi Create using the following command.

Make sure your conda environment is active

pip install -U turicreate

With these steps, you should now be able to successfully install and use Turi Create on your Apple Silicon-based system.

 

Please note that Turi Create is no longer maintained, as the repository was archived by the owner on Dec 21, 2023. It is now read-only.

Good luck.

entry.title: Air Ticket Benefit Carry forward Issue - Resolution Provided
entry.content:
Air Ticket Benefit Carry forward Issue

Business Context: The actual ticket accrual amounts expire when air tickets are accrued over a long period of time, leading to inaccurate ticket accrual balances.

How the employee's ticket accrual was computed and when it expired is shown in the example below. Due to improper parameter declaration at the feature TBPAR, the system is lapsing the balance by year. Consequently, the employee's overall ticket balance should reflect 3, as ticket accrual occurs from 2022 to 2024.

 

Year Begin

 Year End

Ticket Accrual

Remarks

01.01.22

31.12.22

1

Ticket Lapsed in Year 23, if it’s not encashed

01.01.23

31.12.23

1

Ticket Lapsed in Year 24, if it’s not encashed

01.01.24

31.12.24

1

 

Total Ticket

 

1

 Ticket Benefit should have come as 3

TBPAR Feature Specification:

According to the screen print below, if you set the Return value to 12, the system will automatically lapse the ticket benefit accrued by every 12 months, with the expectation that the ticket benefit will be automatically encashed annually. Therefore, there won’t be any carry forward whatsoever.

For the clients that were already gone live with the Feature TBPAR Parameter as 12, this can’t be modified, even if we update the value to 99 this won’t affect the ticket benefit type accrual Process beyond 12 months at the Master data level.

There are genuine business scenarios, where employee may not have encashed his/her ticket benefit hence ticket benefit should get accumulate, rather than lapsing the benefit which is a huge loss to the employee.

Resolution:

Please create a new Ticket Benefit sub type and maintain the return value as mentioned below to extend the ticket benefit accrual carry forward up to 8 years

LEAV is a standard Ticket Benefit Subtype and in this example we have created New ticket benefit type as AIRT

 

Feature TBPAR new return value Maintained in order to carry forward the accumulated Ticket benefit up to 8 Years (96/12=8)

Corrections at a Master Data level:

  1. Create a new Ticket Benefit subtype (In this Example created as AIRT)
  2. Delimit the old subtype in IT3408 and update the master data record with new subtype effective new start Date.
  3. Nullify the balances in the old sub type using Air ticket Correction Info Type 3410

Per Screen Print Above New Ticket Benefit Type Created as AIRT and updated it with an effective date as 01.02.2024

Per the above Screen Print, Old balance lying the old sub type has been Nullified, and the remaining balance has been adjusted in the New Ticket Benefit Type.

entry.title: Re: OpenUI5 webapp, how to logoff from local odataV2 serv...
entry.content:
Hi Jakub,

Why would you try to logout? Any particular reason? If you have your app open, there should be a connection, right?

If you need this for switching users, I'd say have a look at Chrome profiles. You can create multiple users for testing purpose, without closing your application.

So what is your use case to logoff?

Regards,

Noël

entry.title: How to Trigger Process from an App (Video + Free Projects)
entry.content:
Happy to release the first of 4 videos on how to trigger processes from SAP Build Apps using the new Processes flow functions.

 

The benefits of the new flow functions are:

  • No more creating complicated data resources that manually call the API
  • No more duplicating the process schema in your SAP Build Apps project
  • Easily retrieve attributes from the process instance
  • Easily retrieve triggered instances of your process
  • Easily cancel or resume process instances 

The video comes with a free downloadable process and app so you can follow along with the video.

One tip: If you publish a process and update the inputs and redeploy, you must (1) republish the process and (2) update your data resource that points to the process. 

The 4 videos will explain:

  1. How to trigger a process 
  2. How to retrieve instances
  3. How to retrieve instance attributes
  4. How to cancel or resume a running process

entry.title: Re: It is possible collect monthly data monthly only for...
entry.content:
Hi 

Yes you can run quarterly consolidation. You are releasing data for SAP company on monthly basis and for non SAP company you are loading data using flexi upload so all data is available for consolidation.

You define period category for task method which allows Task to use that method every month.

And consolidation frequency allows task to appear in Consolidation monitor.

I hope that answers your question.

Thanks

Niranjan

entry.title: Difference between regacy data and migrated data to S4 in supplier.
entry.content:
We migrated the supplier master data using Migration Cockpit in S4HANA Cloud, we found the item "Bank accountant"(techical name : "KOINH") is different from regacy data which we described in migration template.

Is this item refer to the any other items?
If so, Bank accountant may be entered automatically.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Getting error while configuring SAP system in VSCode
entry.content: Hello jlongie, I have tried the solution earlier but it not works. can you please suggest another solution, Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Getting Request failed with status code 500 using SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Morris,

HTTP error 500 is an internal server error, meaning that something on the other side (Joule) is causing an error. It could be the max number of requests, but then it should give a decent error message back instead of an error 500 (that's what I expect as a developer at least :-))

It also could be that your request contains invalid characters. Could you please try again without the . 

Regards,

Noël

entry.title: Re: Step by Step instruction to Setup / Update IAS User Passwords in Mass using Postman

@meenakshi_si - Please follow my other blog below which detials the steps:

Regards,

Yash

entry.title: Exchange rate at Project builder
entry.content:
Dear,

Background:

1. We use the exchange rate P at project planning cost

2. The project will be complete after several years according to POC. And we will release new exchange rate by yearly for exchange rate P.

Question:

Because the project will cross several years and the exchange rate P will updated yearly, there will be exchange rate fluctuations.

How we can get the fixed exchange rate at project builder?

- Do we have the manually input exchange rate?

-Can we only pick up the exchange rate based on project creation date

Thanks.

entry.title: Capturing moisture losses during storage.
entry.content: Certain materials are getting weight loss (moisture) at the time of storage. In SAP what is the solution that you can propose on this.

entry.title: concatenate hrpad23 fields to validate with dbtablog abap 7.4
entry.content:
I am new to working with SAP Hana and I have not been able to solve the following problem:

I need to obtain the data from the hrpad23 table but only those that are in the dbtablog table. The problem is that to connect both tables I need to CONCATENATE the fields HRPAD23~MANDT and HRPAD23~ADATANR to compare it with the DBTABLOG~LOGKEY field, the limitations of the version abap with opensql does not allow me to CONCATENATE  in the sql statement (select)
I need help to be able to carry out this process in some way.

attentive to your comments.

Thank you

Juan Leiva

entry.title: Re: OpenUI5 webapp, how to logoff from local odataV2 serv...
entry.content:
Nice to see you try our UI5 platform as well as a React developer!

Do you have a standard application or do you use the launchpad functionality as well?

If you are using the launchpad also, you could trigger a logout sap.ushell.Container.logout()

Maybe you can invalidate your session somehow. It is stored in your session cookies indeed. 

(https://www.npmjs.com/package/ui5-middleware-simpleproxy)

I have never seen this use case in UI5 to be honest 🙂

entry.title: SAP HANA Database - Adjust Memory Allocation Limit
entry.content:
Hello.

I have questions about HANA Database Memory Administration.

 

If I change the allocationlimit parameter in the Production tenant DB, do I have to restart that DB?

I wonder if the changes in the parameter can be applied without restart.

My guess is that due to the current limit, delta merge in tables is not being carried out normally.
The error is "column store error: [2484] not enough memory for table optimization".

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: OpenUI5 webapp, how to logoff from local odataV2 serv...
entry.content: Standard webapp, build with easy ui5 generator.

entry.title: Re: Information about SAP BODS and Salesforce API Compati...
entry.content:
Hi,

no, unfortunately this is not possible anymore. The latest supported Version is v21.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003004914

You might want to look at a 3rd party Adapter as mentioned in the note.

Julian

entry.title: how to change the delivery address
entry.content:
We have received the following notice from SAP via Email.
 [SAP Customer Data Cloud] Final Reminder - Deprecation of ECMAScript 5.1 for Dataflow Custom Scripts

I would like to know how to change the delivery address.

entry.title: Automatic Language translations for user texts (PO,SO, FI) during postings
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

As we know global implementations involve cultural and language issues, in a global environment (engaging multiple countries), the requirement is that in a specific country user will be entering texts in local language (say Spanish), but Head office is in English speaking territory. For review purposes or group level accounting postings or central shared services, HO team cannot understand the nature of entry (Line item texts of FI documents, or interpret Sales order texts) entered in the local language (spanish or non-english language). Is there any tool or solution of SAP that will work as Digital translator, and during any document save (PO,SO, accounting documents in particular), it should translate texts to english language also whenever someone logs in with EN language set, but at the same time, if someone logs in with SP (Spanish) language code, spanish text should be available (for the local users). How to manage this scenario?

Regards , 

Azmath.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?

Hello 👏,
I am Gautam, from Germany. I have started to learn SAP. Happy to connect with you all 🙂 Please share your journey and motivation to learn SAP. I would like to hear from you. 🙂

entry.title: Re: connection between sap btp and a s/4 hana cloud priva...
entry.content:
@MaximoSanchez ,

"Do I need to implement the Communication Systems application to connect BTP with my S/4 HANA system?"

Yes, you need to configure Communication Systems in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to establish a connection with SAP BTP. This setup defines the technical details necessary for communication, for example hostname, identity, user information and certificates.

"Do I need Cloud Connector for connecting BTP with S/4 HANA Cloud Private and S/4 HANA on-premise but not for S/4 HANA Cloud Public?"

Correct. With the cloud connector, you are able to connect your on-premises systems and other cloud systems.

entry.title: Re: How to hide table columns dynamically in Fiori Elemen...
entry.content: Hi Sandeep,
